info.name: BX Finance — 3-Token Delegated Chain (may_act / RFC 8693)
info.description:
Tests the full 3-token delegation chain:

**Step 1** — Subject Token via headless PKCE (pi.flow). No browser needed:
  - 1a: GET /authorize with response_mode=pi.flow — PingOne returns a flowId as JSON
  - 1b: POST /flows/{flowId} with username + password — PingOne validates credentials
  - 1c: GET /as/resume?flowId={flowId} — PingOne returns the auth code as JSON
  - 1d: POST /as/token — exchanges code + PKCE verifier for the Subject Token

**Step 2** — RFC 8693 Token Exchange #1. Banking App exchanges Subject Token for MCP Token.
**Step 3** — Client Credentials from BX Finance MCP Service -> PingOne API Token.
**Step 4** — User Lookup via PingOne Management API (proves full chain).

## Collection Variables to fill in

| Variable | Where to find it |
|---|---|
| `PINGONE_ENVIRONMENT_ID` | PingOne Console -> Environment -> Settings |
| `PINGONE_REGION` | `com` (US) or `eu` / `ca` / `ap` |
| `PINGONE_CORE_USER_CLIENT_ID` | Client ID of **BX Finance User** app |
| `PINGONE_CORE_CLIENT_ID` | Client ID of **BX Finance Banking App** |
| `PINGONE_CORE_CLIENT_SECRET` | Client Secret of **BX Finance Banking App** |
| `MCP_CLIENT_ID` | Client ID of **BX Finance MCP Service** |
| `MCP_CLIENT_SECRET` | Client Secret of **BX Finance MCP Service** |
| `TEST_USERNAME` | Username of your test user |
| `TEST_PASSWORD` | Password of your test user |

Run steps in order: **1a -> 1b -> 1c -> 1d -> 2 -> 3 -> 4**. All tokens are auto-saved.

item.name: Step 1 — Subject Token (Headless PKCE via pi.flow)

exec: // Generate PKCE code_verifier (64-char URL-safe random string per RFC 7636)
console.error('[Step 1a] Non-200 response. Not saving flow_id. Response:', JSON.stringify(json));
console.error('[Step 1a] PingOne error payload received. Not saving flow_id. Response:', JSON.stringify(json));
// Prefer the exact credential-submit URL PingOne gives us for this flow
console.warn('[Step 1a] No /flows/ submit URL found in _links; Step 1b will fall back to constructed URL.');
console.error('[Step 1a] No flow id in success payload. Full response:', JSON.stringify(json));

request.description:
Starts the Authorization Code + PKCE flow in headless mode.

`response_mode=pi.flow` tells PingOne to return a JSON flow object instead of redirecting to a browser login page.

The pre-request script generates a fresh `code_verifier` + `code_challenge` (S256).

On success PingOne returns `{"id": "<flowId>", ...}`. The test script saves `flow_id` for Step 1b.

exec: // Some Postman execution contexts (certain runners/monitors) may not expose pm.console.
// Use a safe logger that falls back to console.* when available.
const c = (pm && pm.console) ? pm.console : (typeof console !== 'undefined' ? console : null);
log:  c && typeof c.log === 'function' ? c.log.bind(c) : nop,
warn: c && typeof c.warn === 'function' ? c.warn.bind(c) : (c && typeof c.log === 'function' ? c.log.bind(c) : nop),
error:c && typeof c.error === 'function' ? c.error.bind(c) : (c && typeof c.log === 'function' ? c.log.bind(c) : nop),
throw new Error('[Step 1b] flow_id is empty. Run Step 1a first.');
throw new Error('[Step 1b] flow_id is stale or missing timestamp. Run Step 1a again immediately before Step 1b.');
throw new Error('[Step 1b] TEST_USERNAME is empty. Set it in your active environment or collection variables.');
throw new Error('[Step 1b] TEST_PASSWORD is empty. Set it in your active environment or collection variables.');
throw new Error('[Step 1b] PINGONE_ENVIRONMENT_ID is empty. Set it in your active environment or collection variables.');
// Use PingOne-provided submit URL if Step 1a exposed one
// Log the *resolved* URL (based on the request URL template)
__logger.warn('[Step 1b] Content-Type header is missing; PingOne typically requires a vendor Content-Type for this step.');
__logger.log('[Step 1b] Using PINGONE_REGION=', region, '| PINGONE_ENVIRONMENT_ID=', envId, '| flow_id=', flowId);
__logger.log('[Step 1b] If you still get 404 NOT_FOUND: verify the /as/ path is present, region is correct (com/eu/ca/asia), and Step 1a & 1b are hitting the same env/region.');
// Some Postman execution contexts (certain runners/monitors) may not expose pm.console.
// Use a safe logger that falls back to console.* when available.
const c = (pm && pm.console) ? pm.console : (typeof console !== 'undefined' ? console : null);
log:  c && typeof c.log === 'function' ? c.log.bind(c) : nop,
warn: c && typeof c.warn === 'function' ? c.warn.bind(c) : (c && typeof c.log === 'function' ? c.log.bind(c) : nop),
error:c && typeof c.error === 'function' ? c.error.bind(c) : (c && typeof c.log === 'function' ? c.log.bind(c) : nop),
// Do not hard-fail the run on 404; provide diagnostics.
try { json = pm.response.json(); } catch (e) { json = null; }
__logger.error('  • Wrong flows path (expected .../{envId}/flows/{flowId} for this tenant)');
__logger.error('  • Wrong PINGONE_REGION or PINGONE_ENVIRONMENT_ID (Step 1a and 1b mismatch)');
__logger.error('  • flow_id is stale (rerun Step 1a then Step 1b immediately)');
// Exit early so the normal 200 assertion doesn't fail.
__logger.log('[Step 1b] Flow COMPLETED — run Step 1c to get the auth code.');
__logger.warn('[Step 1b] MFA required. This collection does not handle MFA steps. Disable MFA for the test user or use a bypass policy.');
__logger.log('[Step 1b] Flow status:', json.status, '| Full response:', JSON.stringify(json, null, 2));

request.description:
Submits the user's credentials to the active flow.

The special Content-Type `application/vnd.pingidentity.usernamePassword.check+json` tells PingOne this is a credential submission.

Set `TEST_USERNAME` and `TEST_PASSWORD` in Collection Variables.

On success PingOne returns the updated flow object. The test script checks the status and warns if MFA is required.

item.name: Step 1c — GET /as/resume (get auth code from completed flow)

exec: throw new Error('[Step 1c] flow_id is empty. Run Step 1a and 1b first.');
console.log('[Step 1c] auth_code saved. Run Step 1d immediately (codes expire in ~60 seconds).');
console.error('[Step 1c] No code in response:', JSON.stringify(json));
console.error('[Step 1c] The flow may not be completed. Check Step 1b status.');

request.description:
Resumes the completed flow and retrieves the authorization code.

PingOne returns `{"authorizeResponse": {"code": "..."}}`.

The test script saves `auth_code` for Step 1d.

item.name: Step 1d — POST /as/token (exchange code for Subject Token)

exec: throw new Error('[Step 1d] auth_code is empty. Run Steps 1a, 1b, and 1c first.');
throw new Error('[Step 1d] pkce_code_verifier is empty. Run Step 1a first.');
console.error('[Step 1d] Auth code expired or already used. Rerun Steps 1a -> 1b -> 1c.');
console.log('[Step 1d] Subject Token claims:', JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2));
console.warn('[Step 1d] WARNING: may_act missing. Run Utility — Set mayAct on User (needs Token 3 from Step 3), then rerun Steps 1a-1d.');
console.error('[Step 1d] Could not decode JWT:', e.message);

urlencoded.description: Client Secret of BX Finance User app (required for CLIENT_SECRET_POST token endpoint auth)
Must match the redirect_uri registered on the BX Finance User app

request.description:
Exchanges the PKCE authorization code for the Subject Token (Token 1).

PingOne validates:
- `code` matches the flow that was completed in Steps 1a-1c
- `code_verifier` matches the `code_challenge` sent in Step 1a (PKCE proof)
- `redirect_uri` matches the registered value on the app

The Subject Token will have:
- `aud = https://ai-agent.pingdemo.com` (ENDUSER_AUDIENCE)
- `may_act.sub = PINGONE_CORE_CLIENT_ID` (required for Step 2 Token Exchange)

If `invalid_grant`: the code expired — rerun Steps 1a, 1b, 1c.
If `may_act` missing: run **Utility — Set mayAct on User** (needs Token 3), then rerun Steps 1a-1d.

item.description:
Gets Token 1 (Subject Token) without a browser using PingOne's `pi.flow` headless mode.

Run sub-requests in order: **1a -> 1b -> 1c -> 1d**

Set `TEST_USERNAME` and `TEST_PASSWORD` in Collection Variables before running.

exec: console.log('[Step 2] MCP Token claims:', JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2));
// Verify sub preserved from Subject Token
pm.test('Step 2: sub preserved from Subject Token', () => {
console.error('[Step 2] Could not decode JWT payload:', e.message);

request.description:
RFC 8693 Token Exchange #1.

The **BX Finance Banking App** (`PINGONE_CORE_CLIENT_ID`) exchanges the Subject Token for a narrower MCP-scoped token.

PingOne validates:
- `subject_token.may_act.sub` === `PINGONE_CORE_CLIENT_ID` (the Banking App's own client ID)

On success the returned MCP Token will have:
- `aud = https://mcp-server.pingdemo.com`
- `act.sub = PINGONE_CORE_CLIENT_ID`
- `scope = banking:accounts:read banking:transactions:read banking:transactions:write`

If you get `invalid_grant`: the `may_act.sub` on the user's Subject Token doesn't match `PINGONE_CORE_CLIENT_ID`. Fix by setting `mayAct = { "sub": "<PINGONE_CORE_CLIENT_ID>" }` on the user record (demo app → /demo-data → Enable may_act).

exec: console.log('[Step 3] PingOne API Token claims:', JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2));
// Token 3 should NOT have a sub (service token, not user token)
console.warn('[Step 3] Unexpected sub claim on PingOne API Token:', payload.sub);
console.error('[Step 3] Could not decode JWT payload:', e.message);

request.description:
Client Credentials grant for **BX Finance MCP Service**.

This is NOT a token exchange. The MCP Service authenticates with its own credentials and requests a scoped token for the PingOne Management API.

The returned token will have:
- `aud = https://api.pingone.com`
- `scope = p1:read:user p1:update:user`
- No `sub` (it represents the service, not a user)

If `invalid_scope`: enable `p1:read:user` and `p1:update:user` on the BX Finance MCP Service app Resources tab (Step 2c of setup guide).

exec: pm.test('Step 4: Status 200 — user lookup succeeded', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));
// Check mayAct custom attribute is set
console.log('[Step 4] mayAct on user record:', JSON.stringify(json.mayAct));
console.warn('[Step 4] WARNING: mayAct attribute missing from user record. Token Exchange will fail at runtime. Set it via /demo-data → Enable may_act or via PATCH /users/{id}.');

request.description:
Calls the PingOne Management API using Token 3 (PingOne API Token).

`user_sub` was captured automatically in Step 1 from the Subject Token's `sub` claim.

A 200 response with the user object confirms the full chain works end-to-end:
1. User logged in → Subject Token with `may_act`
2. Banking App exchanged → MCP Token with `act`
3. MCP Service obtained → PingOne API Token with `p1:read:user`
4. Management API authorized the call

Also verifies that the `mayAct` custom attribute is present on the user record.

exec: console.warn('[Introspect] Token is INACTIVE or expired.');

urlencoded.description: Replace with any of: subject_token, mcp_token, pingone_api_token

request.description:
Introspects any saved token against PingOne's introspection endpoint.

Change the `token` body value to one of:
- `{{subject_token}}` — Token 1
- `{{mcp_token}}` — Token 2
- `{{pingone_api_token}}` — Token 3

Or use the [PingIdentity JWT Decoder](https://developer.pingidentity.com/en/tools/jwt-decoder.html) to decode offline.

exec: console.log('[Set mayAct] Now re-run Step 1 to get a fresh Subject Token with the may_act claim.');

request.description:
Sets the `mayAct` custom attribute on the test user.

**Run this if Step 1 returns a Subject Token without a `may_act` claim** (the test script will warn you).

Requires Token 3 (`pingone_api_token`) in the Authorization header — run Steps 1–3 first.

`PINGONE_CORE_CLIENT_ID` is the client ID UUID of **BX Finance Banking App** — the app that is authorized to exchange this user's token.

com | eu | ca | ap

Client ID — BX Finance MCP Service
Client Secret — BX Finance MCP Service
Subject Token audience
MCP Token audience
PingOne API Token audience
AUTO — saved by Step 1 test script after Postman completes the PKCE flow
AUTO — set by Step 2
AUTO — set by Step 3
AUTO — set by Step 1 (user PingOne UUID from sub claim)
Username of the test user (used in Step 1b)
Password of the test user (used in Step 1b)
Client Secret of BX Finance User app — required if token endpoint auth = CLIENT_SECRET_POST
AUTO — generated by Step 1a pre-request
AUTO — generated by Step 1a pre-request
AUTO — saved by Step 1a from pi.flow response
AUTO — saved by Step 1c from resume response
AUTO — credential-submit endpoint from Step 1a _links; Step 1b uses this when available
